title: lawrencia incana
intro: Lawrencia incana, also known as the 'Rough-hair fern', is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Goodeniaceae. It is native to Australia and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote healing. Herbal preparations of Lawrencia incana include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
